steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Any point with $x=0$ lies on the y-axis, so its y-coordinate is the y-intercept.
The line passes through $(0,4)$; therefore the y-intercept is $b = 4$.

steps.title: Write the equation in slope-intercept form
steps.content:
The slope-intercept form is $$y = mx + b$$ with slope $m = -\frac{2}{3}$ and intercept $b = 4$ from step 1.
Substitute: $$y = -\frac{2}{3}x + 4$$.
That matches Choice B — $y=-\frac{2}{3}x+4$.
